human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest, source-policy baseline, scenario suite, evaluation results, deployment notes, and marketing notes for consistency with issue #131 and the repository delivery contract.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to business-case structuring, option appraisal, portfolio prioritization, benefits challenge, and stage-gate decision support.
Real public authoritative sources are documented with publisher, authority reason, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy in the manifest.

The package includes specialist-owned scenarios for governance analysis, commercial-input reasoning, evidence traceability, risk prioritization, low-confidence escalation, adjacent-specialty rejection, and shared-meta-agent fallback.
The manifest defines bootstrap metadata, delegated request and response contracts, and fallback behavior for shared meta-agents required by issue #131.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion is delegated to #225 with safe fallback to the local source baseline.
Scenario normalization, readiness consistency checks, deployment packaging normalization, and commercialization wording review are delegated to #302 through #305 with local-artifact fallback.
Authority-source policy normalization, boundary-routing review, and scaffold consistency review are delegated to #306 through #308 without weakening the specialist-owned baseline.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #131.
Evaluation thresholds exceed the issue minimum with 12 scenarios and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation and adaptation constraints, rollback path, monitoring and logging expectations, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness notes document buyer profile, packaging, subscription model, proof points, and go-to-market gaps without widening scope.
